turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Hello, I'd like to open a new saving account.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! Is this savings account going to be for your personal use, or is it for a joint account with someone else?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Sorry, I don't know what's the difference between usage to account. Could you please describe the function of this?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course! A personal account is just for you alone, so only you can access it. A joint account means you share it with someone else, like a family member, and both of you can manage it. So, would you like it just in your name, or do you want to share it with someone?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Nowadays, I just need to use by myself, so the first one is more suitable for me. And what ID do I need to bring?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! If it's just for yourself, a personal account is perfect. You'll typically need to bring a government-issued ID, like a passport or driver’s license, and maybe proof of address. Does that sound okay?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Okay, I have the Chinese passport, here you are, and I want to know, could I use a foreign phone number or just use a local phone number? Because when I return my hometown, maybe the foreign phone number can't accept the message.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, that’s no problem. We can use your local phone number here so that you’ll still receive messages even when you go back home. So, just give me the local number you’d like to use and we’ll set that up for you.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it, thank you! We'll use that local number for your account. Now let's just fill out the rest of the details and we’ll have your savings account opened up in no time.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “And I want to know should I pay extra money per month or per year for this saving account.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! For a standard savings account, there’s usually no monthly or yearly fee. It’s just free to maintain as long as you keep a small minimum balance. So you won’t have to worry about any extra charges.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “And I want to know, last year I lost a debate card, and I changed it to closed, but now I want to cancel it and get a new one.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, I understand. So you had a debit card that you closed last year, and now you’d like to cancel that closure and get a new one issued. We can absolutely help you with that. We’ll just need to confirm a few details from your previous account, and then we’ll get you set up with a fresh card.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Yes, here is my debate card's number, and could you please post it to China when I return my hometown, or you can just post in Melbourne or in Australia?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, I can help with that. We can definitely arrange to post your new card to China once you’re back home, or we can just mail it to your address here in Australia if that’s easier for you. Just let me know which you prefer.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “I want to proceed to China, and thank you. I will come by this afternoon with my documents.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, we’ll arrange to post it to China for you. Thanks for letting me know, and I’ll see you this afternoon with your documents!
